sections.paragraph:
an unused root. Arabic بَتَّ I. IV. to cut, to cut off, to break off (comp. under the root בָּדַד No. I.); بَتَاتُ something broken off, destroyed; بَتَّةً and البَتَّةَ quite, altogether. In Hebrew it appears to have denoted-

(1) to define, i.e. to measure; whence בַּת a measure.

(2) to cut any thing off, to put an end to a thing, to lay waste altogether, i.q. כִּלָּה; whence בַּתָּה, בָּתָה.
